Records.description: Four-propagator two-loop triangle: three triangle edges with a bubble insertion (two parallel propagators) replacing one of them, yielding four internal lines. The paper presents three different kinematic/mass configurations (varying which external legs are off-shell or massive and which masters are selected) and writes the diagrammatic coaction in each case.
Records.familyName: Two-loop triangle with a doubled edge
Records.location: Section 4, 'We can also explore two-loop cases where the number of propagators is larger. For instance, the diagrammatic coaction of a triangle graph with a double edge are shown for various kinematic configurations below.'

Records.description: Two-loop three-point function with four massless internal propagators forming a triangle in which one side is replaced by a bubble (double edge); all three external legs off-shell. 4 masters (2 top-sector + 2 massless sunset pinches). Masters expressed in Appell F4; diagrammatic coaction in Eq. (4.11). The $p_2^2=0$ limit collapses the top-sector dimension to 2.

Records.authors: Usyukina, Natalia I., Davydychev, Andrei I.
Records.description: Two-loop three-point diagram: triangle with two propagators replacing one internal edge (sunrise/sunset sub-topology on the bottom edge). UV divergent with $1/\varepsilon$ pole. The effective propagator power after bubble integration is $\varepsilon$. Result involves $\Phi^{(1)}$ only.
Records.familyName: triangle with sunrise on edge (fig.1f) / triangle with bubble on edge (fig.1f)

Names: Two-loop triangle with a doubled edge
